Title: To Kill a Mockingbird
Author: Harper Lee
Description: A young girl comes of age in the American South during the 1930s, witnessing her father defend a Black man falsely accused of a crime in a racially charged trial.

Author: George Orwell
Description: In a totalitarian future society, a man begins to question the all-controlling government and falls into a forbidden love affair.

Title: The Great Gatsby
Author: F. Scott Fitzgerald
Description: A mysterious millionaire pursues his lost love against the backdrop of the decadent Jazz Age on Long Island.

Title: Brave New World
Author: Aldous Huxley
Description: A futuristic society engineered for happiness and stability is challenged when a man raised outside its system enters civilization.

Title: The Catcher in the Rye
Author: J.D. Salinger
Description: A disaffected teenager narrates his experiences after being expelled from prep school, wandering New York City and grappling with identity and belonging.

Title: Harry Potter and the Philosopher's Stone
Author: J.K. Rowling
Description: An orphaned boy discovers he is a wizard and begins his education at Hogwarts School of Witchcraft and Wizardry, uncovering his destiny along the way.

Title: The Lord of the Rings
Author: J.R.R. Tolkien
Description: A hobbit embarks on an epic quest with a fellowship of companions to destroy a powerful ring and defeat the dark lord Sauron.

Title: The Hobbit
Author: J.R.R. Tolkien
Description: A homebody hobbit is swept into an unexpected adventure to help a group of dwarves reclaim their mountain homeland from a fearsome dragon.

Title: Dune
Author: Frank Herbert
Description: On a desert planet that produces the most valuable substance in the universe, a young nobleman navigates political intrigue, religious prophecy, and survival.

Title: Foundation
Author: Isaac Asimov
Description: A mathematician develops a plan to preserve civilization across a predicted galactic dark age by establishing a scientific foundation at the edge of the galaxy.

Title: Ender's Game
Author: Orson Scott Card
Description: A child military prodigy is trained at a battle school in space to command Earth's forces against an alien invasion.

Title: The Hitchhiker's Guide to the Galaxy
Author: Douglas Adams
Description: Moments before Earth is destroyed to make way for a hyperspace bypass, an ordinary man is whisked into a wildly absurd journey across the universe.

Title: Neuromancer
Author: William Gibson
Description: A washed-up hacker is hired by a mysterious employer for one last job that takes him deep into the criminal underworld of cyberspace.

Title: The Martian
Author: Andy Weir
Description: An astronaut is stranded alone on Mars after his crew evacuates him for dead, and must use his scientific ingenuity to survive long enough to be rescued.

Title: Murder on the Orient Express
Author: Agatha Christie
Description: Detective Hercule Poirot investigates the murder of a wealthy American businessman aboard the famous Orient Express train.

Title: Gone Girl
Author: Gillian Flynn
Description: On the morning of their fifth wedding anniversary, a woman disappears and her husband becomes the prime suspect in a twisting psychological thriller.

Title: The Girl with the Dragon Tattoo
Author: Stieg Larsson
Description: A disgraced journalist and a brilliant hacker collaborate to solve a decades-old disappearance within a wealthy Swedish family.

Title: In the Woods
Author: Tana French
Description: A Dublin detective investigating the murder of a young girl on an archaeological site finds the case eerily connected to his own traumatic childhood.

Title: The Da Vinci Code
Author: Dan Brown
Description: A Harvard symbologist and a French cryptologist follow a trail of clues hidden in the works of Leonardo da Vinci that leads to a dangerous religious secret.

Title: The Silence of the Lambs
Author: Thomas Harris
Description: An FBI trainee must seek the help of imprisoned cannibal Dr. Hannibal Lecter to catch a serial killer who skins his victims.

Title: No Country for Old Men
Author: Cormac McCarthy
Description: A hunter stumbles upon drug money in the Texas desert and finds himself pursued by a relentless and terrifying hitman across a violent landscape.

Title: The Girl on the Train
Author: Paula Hawkins
Description: A woman who obsessively watches a seemingly perfect couple from her commuter train becomes entangled in a missing persons investigation.

Title: It
Author: Stephen King
Description: A group of childhood friends reunite as adults in their small Maine town to face an ancient shapeshifting evil that preys on children's fears.

Title: Dracula
Author: Bram Stoker
Description: A Transylvanian vampire count makes his way to England to find new blood, opposed by a determined group of heroes led by Professor Van Helsing.

Title: Frankenstein
Author: Mary Shelley
Description: An ambitious young scientist creates a sentient creature from dead body parts, only to abandon it and face the horrifying consequences of his hubris.

Title: The Shining
Author: Stephen King
Description: A struggling writer becomes the winter caretaker of an isolated Colorado hotel, where supernatural forces and his own demons drive him toward madness.

Title: Pride and Prejudice
Author: Jane Austen
Description: The spirited Elizabeth Bennet navigates issues of marriage, morality, and social standing as she squares off against the proud and wealthy Mr. Darcy.

Title: Jane Eyre
Author: Charlotte Brontë
Description: An orphaned governess falls in love with her brooding employer, only to discover a dark secret that threatens to tear their world apart.

Title: Outlander
Author: Diana Gabaldon
Description: A British combat nurse from 1945 is mysteriously transported to 18th-century Scotland, where she falls in love with a Scottish warrior while trying to return home.

Title: Me Before You
Author: Jojo Moyes
Description: A small-town woman becomes a caregiver for a paralyzed former banker, and the two form an unexpected bond that transforms them both.

Title: Sapiens: A Brief History of Humankind
Author: Yuval Noah Harari
Description: A sweeping history of humanity from the emergence of Homo sapiens in Africa to the present day, examining the forces that shaped civilization.

Title: Educated
Author: Tara Westover
Description: A woman raised by survivalist parents in rural Idaho, who never attended school, recounts her journey to Cambridge and Harvard through the power of self-education.

Title: Becoming
Author: Michelle Obama
Description: The former First Lady of the United States reflects on her journey from Chicago's South Side to the White House and her life beyond.

Title: The Immortal Life of Henrietta Lacks
Author: Rebecca Skloot
Description: The story of a Black woman whose cancer cells were taken without her consent in 1951 and became one of the most important tools in medicine.

Title: Thinking, Fast and Slow
Author: Daniel Kahneman
Description: A Nobel Prize-winning psychologist examines the two systems of thought that drive the way we make decisions and form judgments.

Title: A Brief History of Time
Author: Stephen Hawking
Description: A landmark exploration of cosmology covering topics from the Big Bang to black holes, written for general audiences by one of history's greatest physicists.

Title: The Alchemist
Author: Paulo Coelho
Description: A young Andalusian shepherd follows his recurring dream to find treasure, embarking on a journey across the desert that teaches him about the soul of the world.

Title: One Hundred Years of Solitude
Author: Gabriel García Márquez
Description: Seven generations of the Buendía family inhabit the fictional town of Macondo in a sweeping saga blending myth, history, and magical realism.

Title: Crime and Punishment
Author: Fyodor Dostoevsky
Description: A destitute student in St. Petersburg commits a murder he believes is morally justified and then struggles with guilt and psychological torment.

Title: Anna Karenina
Author: Leo Tolstoy
Description: A Russian aristocrat risks her comfortable life by pursuing a passionate love affair, leading to tragic consequences in Tsarist society.

Title: The Brothers Karamazov
Author: Fyodor Dostoevsky
Description: Three brothers with contrasting philosophies are drawn into a murder investigation of their father, raising profound questions about faith, morality, and free will.

Title: Moby-Dick
Author: Herman Melville
Description: The obsessive Captain Ahab leads his crew on a doomed voyage to hunt the great white whale that destroyed his leg, a journey steeped in symbolism and adventure.

Title: The Name of the Wind
Author: Patrick Rothfuss
Description: A legendary wizard recounts his life story to a chronicler, detailing his childhood as a performer, his time at a magical university, and his quest for revenge.

Title: A Game of Thrones
Author: George R.R. Martin
Description: Noble families of the Seven Kingdoms clash for control of the Iron Throne while an ancient supernatural threat stirs beyond the northern border.

Title: The Way of Kings
Author: Brandon Sanderson
Description: On a world ravaged by magical storms, a slave, a young woman, and a powerful soldier are drawn into a conflict that will determine the fate of civilization.

Title: Good Omens
Author: Terry Pratchett and Neil Gaiman
Description: An angel and a demon who have grown fond of life on Earth join forces to prevent the Apocalypse from arriving ahead of schedule.

Title: The Chronicles of Narnia
Author: C.S. Lewis
Description: A group of children discover a magical wardrobe that leads to the enchanted land of Narnia, where they become heroes in an epic battle between good and evil.

Title: Hyperion
Author: Dan Simmons
Description: Seven pilgrims journey to the distant world of Hyperion to face the Shrike, a terrifying creature at the center of the universe's greatest mystery.

Title: Snow Crash
Author: Neal Stephenson
Description: A pizza deliveryman and hacker races to stop a dangerous computer virus that also affects users' brains in a satirical near-future America.

Title: The Left Hand of Darkness
Author: Ursula K. Le Guin
Description: An envoy from a galactic federation travels to a world whose inhabitants are neither male nor female, challenging his assumptions about gender and humanity.

Title: The Road
Author: Cormac McCarthy
Description: A father and son journey through a desolate, post-apocalyptic America, struggling to survive and hold onto their humanity.

Title: The Handmaid's Tale
Author: Margaret Atwood
Description: In the theocratic Republic of Gilead, women are stripped of their rights and forced into reproductive servitude, told through the diary of one such woman.

Title: Fahrenheit 451
Author: Ray Bradbury
Description: In a future America where books are outlawed and burned by firemen, one fireman begins to question the society he serves.

Title: The Hunger Games
Author: Suzanne Collins
Description: In a dystopian future, a teenage girl volunteers to take her sister's place in a televised fight to the death between children selected from twelve impoverished districts.

Title: Sharp Objects
Author: Gillian Flynn
Description: A journalist with a troubled past returns to her hometown to cover the murders of two young girls and uncovers disturbing family secrets.

Title: Big Little Lies
Author: Liane Moriarty
Description: Three women in a seaside Australian town become entangled in a murder investigation connected to secrets they keep about their private lives.

Title: Rebecca
Author: Daphne du Maurier
Description: A young woman marries a wealthy widower and moves to his grand estate, only to feel overshadowed by the lingering presence of his glamorous first wife.

Title: And Then There Were None
Author: Agatha Christie
Description: Ten strangers are lured to an isolated island and begin dying one by one, with no apparent killer among them.

Title: The Firm
Author: John Grisham
Description: A young lawyer discovers that the prestigious firm that recruited him is a front for the mob, trapping him in a deadly web of crime and corruption.

Title: The Bourne Identity
Author: Robert Ludlum
Description: A man is pulled from the Mediterranean Sea with no memory and multiple bullet wounds, and must piece together his identity while being hunted by assassins.

Title: Red Dragon
Author: Thomas Harris
Description: An FBI profiler comes out of retirement to catch a brilliant and savage serial killer, seeking help from the imprisoned Hannibal Lecter.

Title: The Woman in the Window
Author: A.J. Finn
Description: An agoraphobic woman living alone in New York City witnesses a crime through her window, but no one believes her account of what she saw.

Title: Mexican Gothic
Author: Silvia Moreno-Garcia
Description: A glamorous socialite travels to a remote Mexican mansion to rescue her cousin and discovers a dark family secret rooted in sinister supernatural forces.

Title: House of Leaves
Author: Mark Z. Danielewski
Description: A young family moves into a house that is impossible on the inside, and the documentary record of their experience becomes an obsessive maze of footnotes and terror.

Title: Bird Box
Author: Josh Malerman
Description: A mother navigates a post-apocalyptic world while blindfolded, as mysterious creatures cause madness and suicide in any who glimpse them.

Title: The Haunting of Hill House
Author: Shirley Jackson
Description: Four people spend a summer in a notoriously haunted mansion for a paranormal research project, with terrifying results for the most psychically sensitive among them.

Title: The Notebook
Author: Nicholas Sparks
Description: An elderly man reads a love story to a woman with dementia, the tale of two young people from different social worlds who fall deeply in love in 1940s North Carolina.

Title: The Kiss Quotient
Author: Helen Hoang
Description: An econometrician with autism hires an escort to help her learn about relationships, only to fall unexpectedly in love with him.

Title: It Ends with Us
Author: Colleen Hoover
Description: A woman who escaped a difficult childhood finds herself in a complicated relationship that forces her to make the hardest decision of her life.

Title: Normal People
Author: Sally Rooney
Description: Two Irish teenagers from different social backgrounds carry on an on-again, off-again relationship from secondary school through university.

Title: The Body Keeps the Score
Author: Bessel van der Kolk
Description: A pioneering trauma researcher explores how traumatic stress affects the body and brain, and presents innovative treatments to help survivors reclaim their lives.

Title: Atomic Habits
Author: James Clear
Description: A practical guide to building good habits and breaking bad ones by focusing on small, incremental changes that compound into remarkable results over time.

Title: The Power of Now
Author: Eckhart Tolle
Description: A spiritual guide to achieving inner peace by detaching from the incessant chatter of the mind and living fully in the present moment.

Title: Into Thin Air
Author: Jon Krakauer
Description: A first-hand account of the disastrous 1996 Mount Everest expedition in which eight climbers died, exploring ambition, hubris, and the limits of human endurance.

Title: The Diary of a Young Girl
Author: Anne Frank
Description: The journal of a Jewish teenager hiding with her family in Nazi-occupied Amsterdam, offering an intimate record of hope and resilience in the face of persecution.

Title: The Lovely Bones
Author: Alice Sebold
Description: From her place in heaven, a murdered fourteen-year-old girl watches over her family and friends as they grieve and search for her killer.

Title: Life of Pi
Author: Yann Martel
Description: A young Indian boy survives a shipwreck and finds himself stranded on a lifeboat in the Pacific Ocean with a Bengal tiger for 227 days.

Title: The Kite Runner
Author: Khaled Hosseini
Description: A man raised in Kabul grapples with his past betrayal of his childhood friend, ultimately returning to Taliban-controlled Afghanistan to make amends.

Title: A Gentleman in Moscow
Author: Amor Towles
Description: A Russian count is sentenced to house arrest in a luxury Moscow hotel in 1922 and, unable to leave, builds an extraordinary life within its walls over three decades.

Title: Project Hail Mary
Author: Andy Weir
Description: An astronaut wakes up alone on a spacecraft millions of miles from Earth with no memory, and must piece together his mission before humanity goes extinct.

Title: Klara and the Sun
Author: Kazuo Ishiguro
Description: An artificial friend with extraordinary observational powers waits in a shop window to be chosen, narrating the world outside with tenderness and growing unease.

Title: The Three-Body Problem
Author: Liu Cixin
Description: A Chinese astrophysicist makes contact with an alien civilization on the verge of collapse, setting off a chain of events that threatens the future of humanity.

Title: Piranesi
Author: Susanna Clarke
Description: A man lives in a surreal, labyrinthine house of infinite halls and tidal statues, slowly uncovering the truth about how he came to be there.

Title: The Night Circus
Author: Erin Morgenstern
Description: Two young magicians are pitted against each other in a mysterious competition set within an enchanting black-and-white circus that appears without warning.

Title: Six of Crows
Author: Leigh Bardugo
Description: A dangerous criminal mastermind assembles a crew of outcasts for an impossible heist that could make them the richest criminals in the world.

Title: Anxious People
Author: Fredrik Backman
Description: A bank robber's accidental hostage situation in a Swedish apartment showing becomes the catalyst for a cast of peculiar strangers to connect in unexpected ways.

Title: Where the Crawdads Sing
Author: Delia Owens
Description: An abandoned girl raises herself in the North Carolina marshes and becomes the prime suspect in the murder of a man she once loved.

Title: A Man Called Ove
Author: Fredrik Backman
Description: A grumpy widower with rigid routines and a death wish has his solitary existence disrupted by a boisterous young family who move in next door.
